---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
library(brms)
library(tidyverse)
library(BayesFactor)
library(BayesianId)
```

```{r}
data("penguins")
test_data = na.omit(penguins) %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), scale))
levels(test_data$island)
```

```{r}
head(penguins)
```

```{r}
fit = brm_indprior(body_mass ~ flipper_len*bill_len + (flipper_len*bill_len | island), 
         data = test_data,
         chains = 2,
         seed = 1212)
```
```{r}
summary(fit)
```


```{r}
library(bayesplot)

mcmc_dens(fit, pars = vars(starts_with("SD")), adjust = 2)
```

```{r}
library(ks)
post <- as_draws_matrix(fit)[, c("b_flipper_len", "b_bill_len")]
fit_kde <- kde(post)

# Evaluate at a point:
point <- c(0.2, -1.0)
predict(fit_kde, x = point)
```

```{r}
post = as_draws_matrix(fit)
colnames(post)
```
```{r}
post %>% as.data.frame() %>% select(c("b_flipper_len", "sd_island__flipper_len")) %>% cor() %>% round(2)
```


```{r}
#library(rvinecopulib)
library(kde1d)

#theta = abs(rnorm(10000, mean = 0, sd = 4))
#print(2*dnorm(0, mean = 0, sd = 4))
theta = rgamma(10000, shape = 1, rate = 1)
print(dgamma(0, shape = 1, rate = 1))
print("")
aprx_dens = kde1d(theta, xmin = 0, mult = 2)
print(dkde1d(0, aprx_dens))
print("")
aprx_dens = kde1d(theta)
aprx_dens_refl = kde1d(-theta)
print(dkde1d(0, aprx_dens) + dkde1d(0, aprx_dens_refl))
print("")
n <- length(theta); h <- 1.06*sd(theta)*n^(-1/5); f0_hat <- (2/(n*h))*sum(dnorm(theta/h))
print(f0_hat)
```

```{r}
#library(kde1d)
## bounded data, log-linear
x <- rgamma(20000, shape = 1) # simulate data
#fit <- kde1d::kde1d(x, xmin = 0, deg = 2, mult = 7) # estimate density
fit <- rkdevine::kde1d(x, xmin = 0, deg = 2, mult = 7) # estimate density
dkde1d(0, fit) # evaluate density estimate
dgamma(0, shape = 1) 
summary(fit) # information about the estimate
plot(fit, col = "blue") # plot the density estimate
curve(dgamma(x, shape = 1), # add true density
  add = TRUE, col = "red",
  from = 0
)
```


```{r}
x <- rgamma(100, shape = 0.5, rate = 3)        # simulate data
fit <- kdevine::kde1d(x, xmin = 0) # estimate density
kdevine::dkde1d(seq(0, 5, by = 1), fit)     # evaluate density estimate
#summary(fit)                       # information about the estimate
plot(fit, col = "blue")            # plot the density estimate
curve(dgamma(x, shape = 0.5, rate = 3),        # add true density
      add = TRUE, col = "red",
      from = 0)
```

```{r}
n = 10000
n_sim = 2000
sim = expand.grid(i = 1:n_sim,
                  package = c("kdevine", "kde1d"))
sim$est = 0.0
shape = 5
rate = 3

for(i in 1:n_sim){
  x = rgamma(n, shape = shape, rate = rate)
  sim[sim$i == i & sim$package == "kdevine", "est"] = kdevine::kde1d(x, xmin = 0) |> kdevine::dkde1d(x = 0)
  sim[sim$i == i & sim$package == "kde1d", "est"] = kde1d::kde1d(x, xmin = 0) |> kde1d::dkde1d(x = 0)
}
sim$error = sim$est - dgamma(0.0, shape = shape, rate = rate)

sim |> dplyr::group_by(package) |> dplyr::summarize(mse = mean(error^2), bias = mean(error))

# CONCLUSION: The package "kdevine" is MUCH better for bounded data, despite being older.
```

```{r}
hist(x)
```


```{r}
library(mvtnorm)

theta = rgamma(1000, shape = 3, rate = 2)
mu = rnorm(1000, mean = -theta, sd = 2)
data.frame(mu, theta) |> cor() |> round(2) |> print()

fit = data.frame(mu, theta) |> kdevine::kdevine(xmin = c(-Inf, 0))
plot(fit)
```

```{r}
drectnorm = function(x, mu, sigma, log = FALSE){
  #dens = (x < 0)*ifelse(log, yes = -Inf, no = 0) + (x == 0)*pnorm(0, mean = mu, sd = sigma, log.p = log) + (x > 0)*dnorm(x, mean = mu, sd = sigma, log = log)
  dens = case_when(x < 0 ~ ifelse(log, yes = -Inf, no = 0),
                   x == 0 ~ pnorm(0, mean = mu, sd = sigma, log.p = log),
                   x > 0 ~ dnorm(x, mean = mu, sd = sigma, log = log))
  return(dens)
}

print(drectnorm(c(-1, 0, 2), mu = 0, sigma = 1, log = TRUE))
```


```{r}
ll = function(x, pars){
  return(sum(drectnorm(x, mu = pars[1], sigma = exp(pars[2]), log = TRUE)))
}
```

```{r}
theta = rnorm(1000, mean = -2, sd = 1)
theta = ifelse(theta > 0, theta, 0)

est = optim(par = c(0, 0), fn = function(pars){-ll(theta, pars)})

print(c(mean(theta), sd(theta)))
print(c(est$par[1], exp(est$par[2])))
```
```{r}
hist(theta)
```




```{r}

```

```{r}
bar = fit_submodels(fit, submodels(formula(fit)))
```

```{r}
library(lme4)
fit_lmer = lmer(body_mass ~ flipper_len*bill_len + (1 | island), 
                data = test_data,
                REML = FALSE)
results = inc_tests(fit_lmer)
```

```{r}
results$inc_tabl
```


```{r}
vcov(results$fit_list[[1]])
```

```{r}
confint(results$fit_list[[1]], method = "Wald")
```
```{r}
print(fixef(results$fit_list[[1]])["flipper_len"] - 1.96*sqrt(vcov(results$fit_list[[1]])["flipper_len", "flipper_len"]))

print(fixef(results$fit_list[[1]])["flipper_len"] + 1.96*sqrt(vcov(results$fit_list[[1]])["flipper_len", "flipper_len"]))
```


```{r}
coef(results$fit_list[[1]])
```

```{r}
fit_lm = lm(body_mass ~ flipper_len*bill_len,
            data = test_data)
inc_tests(fit_lm)
```

```{r}
vcov(fit_lm)
```

```{r}
fit_blmer = blmer(body_mass ~ flipper_len*bill_len + (flipper_len*bill_len | island),
                  data = test_data,
                  REML = FALSE,
                  fixef.prior = normal,
                  cov.prior = gamma)
summary(fit_blmer)
```

```{r}
fit_blmer2 = update(fit_blmer, formula = body_mass ~ flipper_len + bill_len + (flipper_len + bill_len | island))
```
